items.headline: Grant awarded for Mexican Heritage Plaza School of Arts and Culture
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This funding supports cultural programming in the community.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project moves forward with consultant agreement
items.summary: The City Council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project, advancing safety improvements at this busy intersection.

items.headline: City awards contract for street pavement repairs across San José
items.summary: The council approved bids for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project, which will repair and resurface multiple streets throughout the city.

items.headline: City updates Active Transportation Plan with revised scoring criteria
items.summary: Cupertino is advancing Phase 2 of its Active Transportation Plan, which guides bike lanes, pedestrian improvements, and transit connections. The council reviewed edits and new scoring methods that will determine which projects get prioritized and funded.

items.headline: City Council introduces ordinance to update vacancy-filling rules
items.summary: The council introduced an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are handled and reduce an unnamed item. The agenda summary cuts off before details, making it unclear what substantive change residents should know about.

items.headline: 65-unit senior apartment complex approved for East St John
items.summary: Trillium is building a nearly $9M senior housing development with 65 apartments at 678 E St John St, addressing San José's shortage of dedicated affordable senior housing. This is one of the larger senior housing projects approved recently in the city.

items.headline: Garden Gate Coalition lawsuit against city continues in court
items.summary: An ongoing case called Garden Gate Coalition for Mary Avenue Safety v. City of Cupertino is in Santa Clara Superior Court, with the council meeting in closed session to discuss litigation strategy with legal counsel.

items.headline: City launches business amnesty program and retail incentive initiatives
items.summary: Cupertino is rolling out a Business License Amnesty Program targeting shopping centers, launching a Summer of Celebrations shop-local campaign, and offering economic development incentives for small businesses and startups to boost the local economy.
